human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for spa-specific execution boundaries, source quality, sanitation and worker-safety controls, med-spa boundary handling, scenario coverage, commercialization notes, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to spa execution work for bookings, room readiness, sanitation, wet-area handling, and guest-impact workflows and does not claim licensed practitioner, med-spa, safety-signoff, or legal authority.

Authority sources cover spa-specific TDLR operating guidance plus OSHA worker-safety controls, EPA foot-spa sanitation guidance, and CDC wet-area references, so workflow, terminology, evidence, and failure handling are materially changed by the named industry.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete prerequisites, missing completion evidence, med-spa boundary refusals, sanitation rework, room-readiness conflicts, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-source handling, blood-exposure controls, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/beauty-wellness-and-personal-care/spa-operations-specialist/ so spa operations work remains separated from licensed practitioner, med-spa, facilities, safety, and analytics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around med-spa scope, sanitation shortcuts, safety overrides, and unsupported completion.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated spa operations specialist that supports evidence-backed spa execution, exception handling, and completion control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
